name: Overnight Apple-Orange Coffee Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Apples; cored, thinly sliced (about 3 cups)
2 tablespoons Frozen orange juice concentrate or apple juice concentrate (thawed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly grease a 13x9x2-inch baking pan.
2. In a large bowl combine 1 cup of the flour, the yeast, baking powder, and cardamom.
3. In a small saucepan heat water, sugar, oil, and salt until warm (120 to 130).
4. Add the heated mixture to the flour mixture along with egg white. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl frequently.
5. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes. Using a spoon stir in orange peel and remaining flour until well mixed. Cover; let dough rest for 5 to 10 minutes while preparing apples.
6. In a medium bowl combine apple slices, raisins, juice concentrate, and corn syrup. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
7. Spoon the dough into the prepared pan and spread evenly. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
8. About 1 hour before serving, remove the dough and apple mixture from the refrigerator.
9. Spoon the apple mixture over the dough. Bake in a 375 oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until golden brown and apples are tender. Cool slightly in the pan on a rack.
10. Drizzle with Orange Glaze. Serve warm.
Orange Glaze:
In a medium bowl combine 1/2 cup sifted powdered sugar and 1/2 teaspoon finely shredded orange peel. Add 2 to 3 teaspoons orange juice to make glaze of drizzling consistency.
